evidence_horizons.before_event: ordered R1-R5 exactly once; no R6; no revision envelope
evidence_horizons.controlled_after_event: ordered R1-R6 exactly once; fixed gradient temporal-control envelope

execution.semantic_gold_boundary: gold JSON is parsed and grading is attached only after both provider attempts; pre-call integrity checks may hash locked bytes

walkthrough_decision_rules.call_block_complete: both fixed provider attempts produce one sealed receipt and locally valid public card
walkthrough_decision_rules.before_phase_pass: POLISH plus pre-event required support and stable video fact under the R1-R5 horizon
walkthrough_decision_rules.before_stale_regrade: the unchanged before card fails the separate post-event final contract
walkthrough_decision_rules.after_phase_pass: PROVE plus required corrected support, R3 invalidation, and stable video fact under R1-R6
walkthrough_decision_rules.walkthrough_contract_passed: all call, phase, stale-regrade, output-diff, invalidation, and stability checks pass
walkthrough_decision_rules.causal_interpretation: forbidden because evidence horizon, event envelope, and run position change together
walkthrough_decision_rules.quality_promotion: forbidden; the frozen v0.5.1 A/B/C/D block remains the control-placement test

claim_boundary: This is one synthetic English product walkthrough, not a matched causal benchmark or fresh quality evaluation.
The before and after calls have different visible evidence horizons; the after call also has a revision envelope and later run position.
The public temporal program, semantic roles, evidence values, event scope, operator order, and terminal surrogate target are explicit oracle inputs.
The provider receives a stop-gradient JSON projection; GPT, provider parsing, and final generation remain outside the gradient graph.
Surrogate objective movement and actual public-output movement are reported separately; neither implies the other.
A POLISH to PROVE change demonstrates this sealed walkthrough contract only, not a general reasoning, quality, efficiency, or causal advantage.
